page: Microsoft Azure M64m is a memory-optimized server belonging to the MS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8280M processor operating at 2.7 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes dedicated CPU allocation with hyper-threading enabled. Cache configuration consists of 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 38 MB L3 cache. The server is equipped with 1792.0 GB of system memory, yielding 28.0 GB of RAM per core. It also includes 7340 GB of local storage, while offering zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses and no GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data reveals a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics are generally low, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance in the bottom 25% and Geekbench single-core scores in the bottom 10%. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also fall into the bottom 10%. However, the server demonstrates strong multi-threaded capabilities, with bzip3 multi-threaded decompression and large-block read-only memory bandwidth of 182.37 GB/sec both ranking in the top 25%. Multi-core stress-ng, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU scores sit in the middle 50% average range.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the M64m provides high resource density specifically tailored for memory-intensive workloads rather than raw single-threaded compute power. The massive 1.79 TB memory capacity and 7.3 TB of local storage make it suitable for large-scale in-memory databases, heavy database operations, and multi-threaded data processing tasks that can leverage the high uncached memory read bandwidth. Conversely, it is less suited for single-threaded applications or workloads requiring advanced matrix extensions, as features like AMX and AVX-512 VNNI are absent.
description: Microsoft Azure M64m is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8280M processor running at 2.7 GHz. It is configured with 1792.0 GB of RAM, providing 28.0 GB per core, and includes 7340 GB of local storage. Benchmark results show weak single-core performance, with Geekbench single-core scores in the bottom 10%, but strong multi-threaded capabilities, including large-block read-only memory bandwidth of 182.37 GB/sec and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression in the top 25%. This server is designed for memory-intensive applications, large-scale databases, and multi-threaded enterprise workloads that require substantial memory capacity and local storage, though it presents a tradeoff in single-threaded processing efficiency.
og_description: Microsoft Azure M64m is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 64 vCPUs, 1792 GB RAM, and 7340 GB local storage, delivering strong multi-threaded memory bandwidth alongside weaker single-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure M64m is a memory-optimized server with 64 vCPUs, 1792 GB RAM, and 7340 GB local storage, built for large-scale database workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 platform featuring massive RAM capacity and bundled local storage for multi-threaded database workloads.

1792.0 GB of system memory providing 28.0 GB of RAM per core
7340 GB of bundled local storage for large-scale data retention
Strong large-block read-only memory bandwidth of 182.37 GB/sec
Best suited for in-memory databases and multi-threaded enterprise workloads
